title: Don't setup AppArmor provile for privileged pods
body:
This is essentially db218e7162c2 forward-ported to specgen

Signed-off-by: Ralf Haferkamp <rhafer@suse.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon*, *rhafer*, *saschagrunert* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Feel free to lift the hold if we wanna do the test as follow-up.

Let's do the test in a separate PR.

Just create a --privileged container and then inspect it to see if AppArmor is enabled, should be enough.
/hold cancel
Yes, please add tests. I really don't like merging PRs without tests. We have proven to regress on untested code.

> Yes, please add tests. I really don't like merging PRs without tests. We have proven to regress on untested code.

No worries, I spoke with @rhafer about this PR some hours ago and we will think of adding some (at least basic) e2e tests in conjunction with AppArmor.
